151.reason: [This line attempts to free a pointer that may already be NULL after the loop, or double-free if the loop didn't run. Should check if p is not NULL before freeing. But more critically, after the while loop, p points to the last node, and Vertex[i].link == p, so freeing Vertex[i].link here is correct only if p != NULL. However, the real issue is that the loop condition is p->link != NULL, so when loop exits, p is the last node (non-NULL), then we free(Vertex[i].link) which is p, but p was not freed in the loop. So this is actually okay. Not the main bug.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This line attempts to free a pointer that may already be NULL after the loop, or double-free if the loop didn't run. Should check if p is not NULL before freeing. But more critically, after the while loop, p points to the last node, and Vertex[i].link == p, so freeing Vertex[i].link here is correct only if p != NULL. However, the real issue is that the loop condition is p->link != NULL, so when loop exits, p is the last node (non-NULL), then we free(Vertex[i].link) which is p, but p was not freed in the loop. So this is actually okay. Not the main bug.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Double free: after loop, p is NULL or last node. If p was last node, then Vertex[i].link becomes NULL in loop, so this free(NULL) is safe, but if there's only one node, loop doesn't run, then p = Vertex[i].link (non-NULL), then we free(Vertex[i].link) which is same as p, but then we should set to NULL. However, more critically, the deletion logic is flawed because it doesn't handle single-node case correctly. But the bigger issue is that the graph structure is already corrupted by earlier insertion bugs.]
